data.title: Axon Stock Is Rebounding Today: What's Happening?
data.description:
• Axon Enterprise stock is among today’s top performers. Why are AXON shares rallying?

Axon's rebound follows a sharp risk-off tape on Tuesday, when the Nasdaq fell 1.16% and the S&P 500 shed 0.73% after durable goods orders declined 1.4% in February. Traders are watching whether Wednesday's strength can stick after risk-off volatility across the market pressured high-multiple names.

Notable Developments

Goldman Sachs analyst Michael Ng maintained a Buy rating on Axon and lowered its price target to $625 from $720.

Additionally, the broader market is surging today, with the Nasdaq (QQQ) up 2.93% and the S&P 500 (SPY) up 2.37%. Industrials (XLI) are leading sector gains at 3.73%, and Axon's rally is happening in a session where market breadth is strongly positive.

Axon Technical Analysis

At $398.15, Axon is trading 14.8% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA), the stock's average price over the last 20 sessions, which suggests the short-term trend is still pointed down. It's also 24.8% below its 100-day SMA, indicating the intermediate trend remains under pressure even after today's bounce.

The relative strength index (RSI), a momentum gauge, is 24.16, which is consistent with oversold conditions after heavy selling pressure. That oversold reading can coincide with sharp rebounds, but it also reflects how stretched the recent downtrend has become.

  • Key Support: $396.50 — a near-term line where buyers have recently shown up.

Axon’s Background

Axon is building a public safety operating system by integrating hardware devices with cloud software. Its lineup spans cloud-hosted digital evidence management, TASER energy devices, body and in-car cameras, drones and robotic security, plus training tools.

The business is split into Software and Services and Connected Devices, with most revenue coming from Connected Devices (TASER, cameras, drones and accessories/warranties). That mix matters for the stock's reaction to analyst notes because investors often weigh hardware demand and subscription-like software expansion differently when thinking about longer-term growth and valuation.

Analyst Consensus & Recent Actions: The stock carries a Buy Rating with an average price target of $725.71. Recent analyst moves include:

  • Goldman Sachs: Buy (Lowers Target to $625.00) (April 8)
  • UBS: Neutral (Lowers Target to $570.00) (Feb. 25)
  • Barclays: Overweight (Lowers Target to $682.00) (Feb. 25)

Benzinga Edge Rankings: The Benzinga Edge scorecard for Axon highlights its strengths and weaknesses compared to the broader market.

  • Momentum: Bearish (Score: 3.37) — The stock's trend signals remain very weak versus the broader tape.
  • Value: Bearish (Score: 1.07) — The market is pricing in a lot of optimism relative to fundamentals.
  • Growth: Neutral (Score: 64.1) — Growth factors screen as solid, but not strong enough to offset weak momentum.

The Verdict: Axon’s Benzinga Edge signal reveals a growth-leaning story that's currently being weighed down by very weak momentum and a stretched valuation profile. For longer-term investors, the setup reads like "good business, tough chart," where improving trend signals would matter as much as growth execution.

AXON Price Action: Axon shares were up 5.99% at $395.19 at the time of publication on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro.

Image: Shutterstock

data.title: India's Growth Story Draws ETF Spotlight As INDQ Bets On Quality, Momentum Plays
data.description:
The actively managed ETF, launched last week as part of Pacer's Custom ETF Series, targets high-quality Indian companies using a blend of quality, value, and momentum factors — offering investors a differentiated way to access one of the world's fastest-growing major economies.

The strategy, developed in partnership with Mumbai-based ActiveAlpha, draws from the Nifty 500 Index and Nifty Microcap 250 Index. It then narrows this pool down to a concentrated portfolio of 20 to 30 stocks, selected through a composite scoring system designed to identify fundamentally strong companies positioned to benefit from India's structural growth tailwinds, including favorable demographics, rising consumption and infrastructure expansion.

Key features of the ETF:

data.title: Here's How Much You Would Have Made Owning e.l.f. Beauty Stock In The Last 5 Years
data.description:
e.l.f. Beauty (NYSE:ELF) has outperformed the market over the past 5 years by 7.5% on an annualized basis producing an average annual return of 17.84%. Currently, e.l.f. Beauty has a market capitalization of $3.83 billion.

Buying $100 In ELF: If an investor had bought $100 of ELF stock 5 years ago, it would be worth $226.51 today based on a price of $64.17 for ELF at the time of writing.

e.l.f. Beauty's Performance Over Last 5 Years

Finally -- what's the point of all this? The key insight to take from this article is to note how much of a difference compounded returns can make in your cash growth over a period of time.

data.title: Why Is IREN Stock Jumping Wednesday?
data.description:
IREN Ltd (NASDAQ:IREN) shares are climbing during Wednesday’s session. The move comes as global markets react to a significant geopolitical shift.

• IREN stock is surging to new heights today. What’s behind IREN gains?

Geopolitical De-Escalation Boosts Sentiment

Bitcoin Surges Past Key Levels

Caution Remains Despite Truce

While markets soared, Tehran offered a more measured tone. Iran's Supreme National Security Council stated, “Our fingers are on the trigger; any mistake by the enemy will be met with a full-scale response.”

Technical Analysis

At $36.81, IREN is trading 4.8% below its 20-day simple moving average (SMA), the stock’s average price over the last 20 sessions. It's trading 16.3% below its 100-day SMA.

The moving average convergence divergence (MACD), a trend/momentum measure, is bearish with the MACD at -2.2387 versus a -1.9233. The 50-day SMA remains above the 200-day SMA (a longer-term constructive backdrop), even as the 20-day SMA sits below the 50-day SMA, keeping the shorter-term picture mixed.

Over the past 12 months, the stock is up 601.32%. The current price is well off the $76.87 52-week high and far above the $5.13 52-week low.

  • Key Resistance: $43.50
  • Key Support: $35.50

IREN Stock Price Activity: Iren shares were up 4.42% at $37.32 at the time of publication on Wednesday, according to Benzinga Pro data.

Image via Shutterstock

data.title: 'Anthropic is Eating Palantir's Lunch': Michael Burry
data.description:
• PLTR stock is down. See the chart and price action here. 

Burry Strengthens His Case Against PLTR

Burry posted his latest jab at Palantir Wednesday on X: 

"Anthropic is eating $PLTR Palantir’s lunch. That massive boost from $9B to $30B ARR at Anthropic is because Anthropic offers the easier, cheaper, intuitive solution for businesses," he said.  

"PLTR can have government, which is low margin and small.  Anthropic’went from $9B to $30B in months, it took $PLTR 20 years to get to $5 Billion.  Anthropic is taking 73% of all new enterprise spending per Ramp," Burry added. 

What Ramp's Data Actually Shows 

Ramp's March AI Index shows business AI adoption at a record 47.6%, with nearly one in four Ramp customers now paying for Anthropic, up from roughly one in 25 a year ago. 

Anthropic's adoption grew 4.9 percentage points month-over-month, its fastest pace yet, and it now wins about 70% of first‑time, head‑to‑head enterprise purchasing decisions versus OpenAI.

Ramp also notes this is a "complete reversal" from 2025, when OpenAI was gaining share faster than rivals, underscoring a rapid shift toward Claude in business workflows. 

That backdrop makes Burry's claim that Anthropic is "eating Palantir's lunch" resonate with data showing Anthropic capturing the bulk of incremental enterprise AI spend.

Burry's PLTR History

Burry previously disclosed a sizeable short in Palantir via long‑dated $50 strike put options expiring in 2027, giving him downside exposure to roughly 5 million shares. 

He has argued Palantir is a low‑margin consulting shop dressed up as a high‑growth AI/SaaS story, pointing to accounts receivable expanding far faster than revenue and heavy stock‑based comp as red flags.

He has also blasted what he calls Palantir's "overpromising" on AI, saying the company relies on external model providers like Anthropic and "has no real AI software of its own." 

Burry's latest post builds on his view that Palantir's government work is smaller, lower‑margin and increasingly dependent on the very foundation models that are now winning direct enterprise relationships on their own.

Burry Still Bearish 

Ramp's numbers give Burry fresh ammunition: Anthropic is rapidly becoming the default choice for corporate AI spend, while Palantir must prove its higher‑touch platforms can capture comparable economics. 

If Anthropic continues to dominate new enterprise AI budgets, Burry's bet is that the market will re-rate Palantir away from pure‑play AI darling toward more mundane systems integrator multiples.

PLTR Price Action: According to Benzinga Pro data, Palantir stock was down 2.05% at $146.98 Wednesday, amid a broad market rally on the Middle East ceasefire. 

This image was generated using artificial intelligence via Gemini.
